41.reason: [The logic for detecting end of first polynomial input is flawed. It assumes exponents are strictly decreasing and uses an arbitrary initial t, which may not work for all valid inputs. Also, it doesn't check for EOF or newline, leading to incorrect parsing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The logic for detecting end of first polynomial input is flawed. It assumes exponents are strictly decreasing and uses an arbitrary initial t, which may not work for all valid inputs. Also, it doesn't check for EOF or newline, leading to incorrect parsing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition breaks on increasing exponent, but input is in descending order; should break when exponent increases relative to previous, but logic is inverted and initial t is too large causing immediate break]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The logic for detecting end of first polynomial input is flawed. It assumes exponents are strictly decreasing and uses an arbitrary initial t, but input may not follow this pattern or may have equal exponents (though problem says non-zero terms only, order isn't guaranteed to be strictly descending in practice for breaking condition). Also, after the first polynomial ends, xi and zhi retain last values and are reused incorrectly in the next loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition `t < zhi` is incorrect for detecting end of first polynomial input. The input ends with a newline, not by increasing exponents. This causes premature termination or infinite loop depending on input order.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The logic for detecting end of first polynomial input is flawed. It assumes exponents are strictly decreasing and uses an arbitrary large t, but input may not follow this pattern or may have equal exponents (though problem says non-zero terms only, order isn't guaranteed to be strictly decreasing in all test cases). Also, after the first polynomial ends, the last read (xi, zhi) belongs to the second polynomial but is used in the next loop before re-reading.]

47.reason: [At this point, 'xi' and 'zhi' hold the last values from the first polynomial input loop, not from the second polynomial. This incorrectly uses stale data to start building x2 before reading the second polynomial.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Uses stale 'xi' and 'zhi' from the last read of the first polynomial as if they belong to the second polynomial. This is completely wrong: the second polynomial hasn't been read yet at this point.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [`xi` and `zhi` here are leftover from the last read of the first polynomial, not from the second polynomial. This line should not exist before reading the second polynomial.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Here, 'xi' and 'zhi' are from the first term of the second polynomial that was read during the break condition, but this loop runs before reading the rest of the second polynomial. This causes incorrect partial multiplication using only one term of the second polynomial.]

49.reason: [After first polynomial input fails (due to newline), this tries to read second polynomial, but previous loop already consumed part of input incorrectly]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This attempts to read the second polynomial, but the previous loop already tried to process multiplication using uninitialized second polynomial data.
